section.heading: Rest and recuperation absence: certain members undergoing extended deployment to a combat zone

subsection.heading: Rest and Recuperation Authorized.—
subsection.content: Under regulations prescribed by the Secretary of Defense, the Secretary concerned may provide a member of the armed forces described in subsection (b) the benefits described in subsection (c).

subsection.heading: Covered Members.—
subsection.chapeau: A member of the armed forces described in this subsection is any member who—

paragraph.chapeau: is assigned or deployed for at least 270 days in an area or location—

subparagraph.content: that is designated by the President as a combat zone; and
in which hardship duty pay is authorized to be paid under section 305 or 352(a) of title 37; and

paragraph.content: meets such other criteria as the Secretary of Defense may prescribe in the regulations required by subsection (a).

subsection.heading: Benefits.—
subsection.chapeau: The benefits described in this subsection are the following:

paragraph.content: A period of rest and recuperation absence for not more than 15 days.
Round-trip transportation at Government expense from the area or location in which the member is serving in connection with the exercise of the period of rest and recuperation.

subsection.heading: Construction With Other Leave.—
subsection.content: Any benefits provided a member under this section are in addition to any other leave or absence to which the member may be entitled.

section.sourceCredit: (Added Pub. L. 111–383, div. A, title V, § 532(a), Jan. 7, 2011, 124 Stat. 4216; amended Pub. L. 115–91, div. A, title VI, § 618(e), Dec. 12, 2017, 131 Stat. 1426.)
